summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch must handle minor Claude JSON key drift and fail closed with explicit blockers on invalid agent JSON.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
risks: Malformed provider output could be silently accepted or produce unclear blockers that hide readiness failure.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements JSON repair for minor Claude key drift and fails closed with explicit EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blockers on unrecoverable JSON, keeping PRD readiness blocked.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function repairs common Claude JSON drift including unquoted line numbers, backslash escapes, duplicate keys, and trailing commas.
evidence.why: This is the core repair logic that handles minor recoverable JSON drift.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function extracts JSON from provider output with fenced code block fallback and slicing to find JSON boundaries.
evidence.why: This handles JSON embedded in markdown or mixed with other text.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult function returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null or provider fails, and EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED when evidence records lack scope field.
evidence.why: This is the fail-closed logic that blocks on unrecoverable JSON.

evidence.summary: Test repairs minor Claude JSON key drift verifies line value format changes, backslash escape patterns, and extra quotes in key names are repaired and recorded.
evidence.why: This test proves minor drift is recoverable and recorded.

evidence.summary: Test invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code verifies that when provider returns non-JSON output, agent_result has EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON and readiness blockers include it.
evidence.why: This test proves unrecoverable JSON fails closed with explicit blockers.

evidence.summary: Test agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization verifies EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error code when evidence records lack scope field.
evidence.why: This proves the validation catches missing required fields.

evidence.summary: Stress test ledger shows real Claude agent returning EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON with Unexpected token error, confirming the fail-closed behavior in production.
evidence.why: This is empirical evidence from actual stress testing with real Claude agents.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime function builds boundary snapshots before and after agent execution, detecting violations and adding BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers.
evidence.why: This shows the boundary enforcement that prevents project mutation during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: git status shows src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts and __tests__/demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts are untracked files in a dirty working tree.
evidence.why: This confirms the status_quo claim about existing code in a dirty working tree.

risks: If JSON repair logic is too permissive, malformed provider output could be silently accepted
If EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON is not propagated to readiness blockers, PRD readiness could incorrectly pass despite failed agents

result.notes: Claim verified: evidence-dispatch.ts implements JSON repair for minor drift at lines 330-346, returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON for unrecoverable cases at lines 370-421, and test coverage validates both scenarios. Stress test artifacts confirm real-world behavior matches implementation.

agent_results.claim: All 10 evidence records from the explorer have been verified against actual file contents; no assumptions were presented as facts, all paths are usable and accurate, and no missing data remains.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function verified: contains regex replacements for line number format, backslash escapes, duplicate keys, and trailing commas.
evidence.why: Direct verification of explorer claim about JSON repair logic.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function verified: implements fenced code block parsing and JSON boundary slicing.
evidence.why: Direct verification of explorer claim about JSON extraction.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult function verified: line 372 returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON when parsed is null, lines 408-419 return EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED on missing scope.
evidence.why: Direct verification of explorer claim about fail-closed logic.

evidence.summary: Test repairs minor Claude JSON key drift verified: injects common drift patterns and asserts json_repaired is true.
evidence.why: Direct verification of explorer claim about repair test coverage.

evidence.summary: Test invalid agent JSON fails closed verified: returns non-JSON output, asserts EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON in agent_results and readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Direct verification of explorer claim about fail-closed test.

evidence.summary: Test agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked verified: removes scope field, asserts EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error code.
evidence.why: Direct verification of explorer claim about scope validation test.

evidence.summary: Stress test ledger verified: iteration 1 shows explorer role with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blocker and Unexpected token error message.
evidence.why: Direct verification of explorer claim about real-world stress test evidence.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime boundary snapshot logic verified: function creates before/after snapshots and detects violations.
evidence.why: Cross-check of explorer claim about boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: git status verified: src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts and __tests__/demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts appear in git status output as untracked files.
evidence.why: Verification of explorer claim about dirty working tree status quo.

risks: If JSON repair regex patterns become too permissive, severely malformed JSON could be accepted as valid
If EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blocker is not propagated to readiness.blockers, PRD readiness could incorrectly pass

result.notes: All explorer evidence verified against actual file contents. No assumptions presented as facts. All line references accurate. Stress test artifact confirms real-world behavior matches implementation. No missing data remains. Claim is fully supported by project-scoped evidence.
